issues.originalText: Code Example: Validated Finance Tracker with Pydantic
issues.description: CRITICAL CONTENT MISALIGNMENT: Lesson 02 folder contains Pydantic v2 content instead of class attributes/methods content. The entire content/02-example.md shows Pydantic patterns, not class attributes or methods. This makes the lesson completely wrong for its stated topic.
issues.webResearchNote: N/A - Content structure error, not version issue

issues.description: Missing correct content for class attributes and methods lesson. Challenge starter/solution files also use Pydantic-style dataclass patterns instead of class attributes/methods patterns.

issues.description: CRITICAL CONTENT MISALIGNMENT: Lesson 03 folder contains polymorphism content instead of inheritance content. issues.originalText:
# Note: This example shows Pydantic v2 patterns
# Install: pip install pydantic>=2.0
issues.description: The example uses a simulated Pydantic implementation using @dataclass instead of actual Pydantic BaseModel. While this teaches concepts, it doesn't show real Pydantic v2 code. The comments show what real Pydantic would look like, but learners may be confused.
issues.webResearchNote: Pydantic v2.10+ is current stable. Content uses simulation pattern which is pedagogically valid but may cause confusion. Real Pydantic code should ideally be shown in runnable form.

issues.description: Version pin '>=2.0' is very broad. Current Pydantic is v2.10+. The version-manifest.json specifies Pydantic 2.x as target, so this is acceptable but could be more specific.
issues.webResearchNote: Pydantic 2.10.6 is latest stable (Feb 2026). The >=2.0 specification is valid per version-manifest.json which targets 2.x.

criticalFinding: SEVERE CONTENT MISALIGNMENT DETECTED: Lessons 02, 03, and 04 have their content swapped. Lesson 02 (Class Attributes/Methods) contains Pydantic content. Lessons 03 (Inheritance) and 04 (Polymorphism) have their content swapped with each other. This makes 3 of 9 lessons completely incorrect for their titles.
